raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX I signed a XXXX personal training membership at $ XXXX. I made timely payments on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX I exercised a written cancellation right under an express clause in the signed membership agreement. No payments were missed or refused. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, without a payment default which is the contractual precondition for the remedy applied the merchant charged {$4900.00}, representing the remaining 9 months accelerated as a lump sum. The merchant then continued billing $ XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, attempting to collect the same balance twice. Amex credited the XXXX monthly charges but upheld the {$4900.00}. 
In the dispute response, the merchant misrepresented his own contract 's cancellation clause to Amex, characterizing it as applying only to gender composition changes. The contract explicitly states the clause applies to substantial changes 'including but not limited to " that example. Amex accepted this mischaracterization without independent review of the contract language. 
The merchant 's own document submitted to Amex his service description lists monthly body composition reevaluation as a committed deliverable. It was never delivered. Amex had this document and did not act on it. 
Total charges of {$7600.00} exceed the full contract value of {$6500.00} by {$1000.00}.
